g.text: Valid Escape vs Unknown Escape — What Changes at Step 5

tr.td: advance() → not ", not \n, not \ → continue loop
ch == '\\' branch hit; is_at_end()? No → call advance() for payload
escape_char = 'n'; 'n' ∈ VALID_ESCAPE_CHARS → valid; stay in loop (no token)

div.p:
Result: 1 token
STRING('"hello\\nworld"', line=1, col=1)
Lexeme is raw source text — backslash + n, not actual newline byte.
Escape interpretation is downstream.

tr.td: ch == '\\' branch; is_at_end()? No → call advance() for payload
DO NOT return — error recovery: loop continues

div.p: Result: 2 tokens (error recovery — both appear in stream)

ol.li: ERROR('"bad\\q', line=1, col=1) — position is opening ", not the \

STRING('"bad\\qword"', line=1, col=1) — full string still emitted
Key invariant: _scan_string() does NOT return after the bad escape.
The loop continues, finds the closing ", and emits the STRING token.

div.p: python escape_char = advance() # 'n' if escape_char not in # 'n' IN set VALID_ESCAPE_CHARS: # → condition FALSE append(ERROR...) # skipped # no return #
python escape_char = advance() # 'q' if escape_char not in # 'q' NOT IN set VALID_ESCAPE_CHARS: # → condition TRUE append(ERROR...) # ← ERROR appended # NO return here! # ← recovery: continue
